programme.desc: Alma le pregunta a Franco si está seguro de terminar con ella Diego asegura que su papá está en Nueva York y no sabrá que están de gira Miguel no sabe cómo decirle a Mia que Sabrina es la fotógrafa oficial de RBD Sol abandona a Celina en la selva.

programme.title: Rebelde
programme.desc: Mia se entera que Sabrina estará en el concierto de RBD en Monterrey y se entristece Max llega a Puerto Vallarta y, al ver a Celina, le dice que la quiere. Roberta le dice a Mia que no permita que Sabrina le quite las ganas de cantar.

programme.title: Rebelde
programme.desc: José y Celina se asustan al ver a Javier y al enterarse que León está en Puerto Vallarta el concierto de RBD fue un éxito, y Mia le agradece a Roberta sus consejos Javier le dice a León que Diego esta con una chica para distraerlo.

programme.title: Rebelde
programme.desc: Los chicos de RBD sufren porque en el avión en el que viajan tienen unas terribles turbulencias provocadas por una tormenta.

programme.title: Rebelde
programme.desc: Los muchachos de RBD llegan a Puerto Vallarta para reunirse con los otros estudiantes del colegio. Los estudiantes conviven haciendo diversas actividades en la playa. Diego reta a Javier a una carrera en moto acuática y el ganador se queda con Rob.

programme.title: Tres veces Ana: El único que sabe
programme.desc: Mariano se molesta con Ana Leticia por verse con Ramiro a sus espaldas. Orlando le dice a Ramiro que Ana Lucía está viva y que él es el único que puede encontrarla.

programme.title: Pasión
programme.desc: Mario le cuenta a Jimena que Ricardo es espía del rey. Ricardo le confiesa a Jimena que está enamorando de ella.

programme.title: Pasión
programme.desc: Doña Camila queda fascinada con el beso de don Ricardo. Ofelia le dice a Rita que está embarazada. Doña Lisabeta tiene la esperanza de que don Ricardo algún día se case con ella.

programme.title: Pasión
programme.desc: Don Ricardo le cuenta a Mario que le propuso matrimonio a Jimena. Don Alberto le pide a Vasco que investigue todo sobre don Ricardo. Don Marcelino visita a Sofia y ella le pregunta más sobre Ofelia.

programme.title: Pasión
programme.desc: Ascanio le confiesa a Mario quiénes son los autores intelectuales de la muerte por la que culpan a don Ricardo. Mario le cuenta a Ricardo que Alberto y Vasco están muy interesados en él. Doña Camila habla con Ascanio para que ponga su negocio.

programme.title: Pasión
programme.desc: Santiago le ofrece a Ascanio que se case con Inés. Don Jorge le propone a Justo que case a Inés con Gonzalo.

programme.title: Esmeralda
programme.desc: Paulina descubre que Paola se encuentra en Acapulco y ha estado utilizando su dinero. Estela continúa vigilando a Willy a petición de Estefanía. Paulina le propone a Willy darle un auto nuevo si la ayuda a salvar a Estefanía.

programme.title: Esmeralda
programme.desc: El mismo día nacen dos bebés, un varón y una niña que son intercambiados de familia. Luego de 20 años, el destino hará que Esmeralda y José Armando se enamoren, lo que será el principio del calvario que deberán sufrir para poder concretar felicidad.

programme.title: El chivatazo
programme.desc: Varias organizaciones llegan al extremo donde el conflicto entre capos combina el odio, la traición, y la venganza.

programme.title: El futbolista fenómeno
programme.desc: Un vendedor de cerveza es secuestrado por unos extraterrestres que le otorgan poderes para ser un excelente futbolista.

programme.title: Balún Canán
programme.desc: Unos brujos condenan a una mujer a un destino feroz porque hizo cosas crueles para conservar su finca.

programme.title: Santo y Blue Demon en la Atlántida
programme.desc: Un científico nazi, usando la ciudad perdida de Atlantis como su base de operaciones, amenaza con destruir la civilización con una bomba nuclear. El científico nazi quiere que el Tercer Reich empiece a gobernar de nuevo y si sus demandas no se cumplen, él seguirá adelante con su amenaza de aniquilación nuclear. Con muy poco tiempo que perder, Santo es enlistado para encontrar la bomba nuclear, desarmarla y poner fin al plan diabólico del científico nazi.

programme.title: Cabo de Hornos
programme.desc: El capitán de un barco ballenero conoce a una joven hermosa en el puerto de Valparaíso e inician un romance apasionado.

programme.title: El que tenga un amor
programme.desc: Víctor debe dejar la vida de fiesta para casarse en un matrimonio arreglado. Lucero, su novia, le pide a un amigo de Víctor que lo rescate.

programme.title: Dos corazones y un cielo
programme.desc: El ego mutuo entre una pareja de artistas provoca rencillas y separación, pero un cómico primo tiene la solución.

programme.title: Corazón salvaje
programme.desc: Una mujer abandona en la noche de bodas a su marido para huir con un contrabandista y desencadena una tormenta pasional.

programme.title: Como dice el dicho
programme.desc: Un joven se vuelve líder de una banda de delincuentes por querer ayudar a su familia. Su hermano lo descubre y quiere trabajar con él para salir de la miseria pero el final trágico de su hermano lo ayudará a retomar el buen camino.

programme.title: Como dice el dicho
programme.desc: Un joven se dedica a vender drogas en su escuela, un profesor se entera y lo denuncia. El joven les cuenta a los hombres que le venden la droga sobre su profesor y ellos lo golpean hasta dejarlo inconsciente.

programme.title: Como dice el dicho
programme.desc: Una joven sufre de violencia familiar. La joven está harta de la situación en la que vive y trata de convencer a su madre de que huyan de su casa.

programme.title: Como dice el dicho
programme.desc: Una señora le pide a su hija que se vaya de la casa porque su patrona cree que ella le robó dinero. Cuando la chica va a su casa un hombre la secuestra, la lleva a una casa y trata de prostituirla.

programme.title: Como dice el dicho
programme.desc: Un chico se vuelve adicto a fumar sin saber que eso le traerá grandes problemas con sus sueños de ser un gran jugador de fútbol, además de hacerle daño a su hermano menor ocasionándole una gran enfermedad.

programme.title: Como dice el dicho
programme.desc: Un niño se orina en sus pantalones y sus padres tienen que cambiarlo de escuela. El niño descubre que el compañero de clase que lo hostiga con saña brutal también padece de enuresis.

programme.title: Como dice el dicho
programme.desc: Una jovencita es golpeada por su padre, y para proteger a su madre, no se queja de nada. La mamá de la niña se la pasa tomando pastillas para dormir y descuida a su hija. La maestra se da cuenta de que es maltratada por sus padres.

programme.title: Como dice el dicho
programme.desc: Una chica va caminando con un amigo de la escuela de regreso a su casa, cuando el novio de su mamá los encuentra en la calle y amenaza al chico para que se aleje de ella. Cuando llegan al departamento, el novio de la mamá intenta manosear a la chica.

programme.title: Oscar Peterson Trio, Stuff Smith & Ella Fitzgerald
programme.sub-title: O.P. Trio and E. Fitz. - Jazz At The Philharmonic
programme.desc: The Royal Concertgebouw in Amsterdam is a world-renowned concert hall, known for its first-rate acoustics. It has attracted many famous performers over the years and is one of the Netherlands' most treasured musical institutions. On May 5, 1957, jazz impresario Norman Granz presented his Jazz at the Philharmonic tour at the Concertgebouw in Amsterdam. The Canadian pianist Oscar Peterson and his trio of guitarist Herb Ellis and bassist Ray Brown are later joined by trumpeter Roy Eldridge and drummer Jo Jones. As an added attraction, American violinist Stuff Smith joins the proceedings. American singer Ella Fitzgerald, accompanied by pianist Don Abney, Ellis, Brown and Jones, hits her vocal peak in a stunning set that culminates in a jam session of It Don't Mean A Thing (If It Ain't Got That Swing) with the entire ensemble.

programme.title: Charlie Galbraith
programme.desc: An extraordinary collection of clips in all jazz genres from the most beautiful concerts and the most special festivals, performed by the finest jazz musicians.

programme.title: Maceo Parker
programme.desc: As a 25-year-old saxophonist, Maceo Parker played in the band of James Brown, but he is also known for his collaborations with George Clinton's Parliament-Funkadelic and Bootsy Collins' Rubber Band. Maceo has proven his star quality by creating a special chemistry between all the different styles of American black music. As a pioneer in the ‘groove', he blends funky jazz with roots and blues with gospel. The result? Overheated concert halls, with enthusiastic audiences that completely surrender themselves to the music's boundless energy.

programme.title: Chet Baker
programme.desc: An extraordinary collection of clips in all jazz genres from the most beautiful concerts and the most special festivals, performed by the finest jazz musicians.

programme.title: Chick Corea, John Patitucci & Dave Weckl
programme.sub-title: Chick Corea Akoustic Band - Münchner Klaviersommer
programme.desc: 'Münchner Klaviersommer' was an annual concerts series that took place from 1981 to 1998 in Munich, Germany. Although the festival's name suggests a strong focus on piano music, it featured countless famous musicians from jazz and classical music – not just pianists. The concerts were usually held in July at The Gasteig, home of the Munich Philharmonic. In 1989, American pianist Armando Anthony "Chick" Corea was one of the artists appearing here. In this concert Corea returned to his roots, performing jazz standards and originals in a stripped-down trio setting of piano, bass and drums. With Dave Weckl (drums) and John Patitucci (bass), this highly interactive trio actually had its beginnings in 1985 with Chick Corea's Elektric Band.

programme.title: Chucho Valdis
programme.desc: Legendary Cuban pianist Chucho Valdés brought his band ‘Jazz Batá 2' to the Malta Jazz Festival on July 19, 2019. The air was thick with anticipation as Valdés took to the stage, revisiting the small-group concept of his mythical 1972 Cuban album ‘Jazz Batá'. The batá repertoire – the deep classical music of West Africa – permeates Valdés' piano solos throughout the concert. Valdés's band consists of Dreiser Durruthy (Batas and vocals), Yaroldy Abreu (percussions and vocals), Abraham Mansfarroll (percussion), Ramón Vazquez Martirena-Bajo (bass guitar). They delivered a performance that reverberated through the historic island.

programme.title: John Coltrane
programme.sub-title: I want to talk about you
programme.desc: Explore the music of one of the most influential and innovative saxophonists in jazz history: John Coltrane! Immerse yourself in this collection of remarkable performances from the early 1960s that showcase Coltrane's unparalleled artistry and unique sound during a transformative period in the artist's career. Recorded in various European cities, Coltrane is seen playing the songs “Autumn Leaves”, “Hackensack”, “My Favorite Things”, “I Want To Talk About You”, “Vigil”, and “Naima”. Tune in and experience why the music of John Coltrane has touched the lives of so many!

programme.title: Lionel Hampton
programme.desc: An extraordinary collection of clips in all jazz genres from the most beautiful concerts and the most special festivals, performed by the finest jazz musicians.

programme.title: Joe Zawinul, Steve Khan, Victor Bailey & Peter Erskine
programme.desc: 'Münchner Klaviersommer' was an annual concerts series that took place from 1981 to 1998 in Munich, Germany. Although the festival's name suggests a strong focus on piano music, it featured countless famous musicians from jazz and classical music – not just pianists. The concerts were usually held in July at The Gasteig, home of the Munich Philharmonic. In 1986, jazz-fusion band “Weather Update” was one of the bands appearing here. This short-lived band was formed by Austrian keyboardist Joe Zawinul in 1986, following the breakup of his famous “Weather Report” band. Again led by Zawinul, Weather Update consisted of Victor Bailey on bass, Peter Erskine and Robert Thomas Jr. on percussion instruments, and Steve Khan on guitar.

programme.title: London Symphony Orchestra, Gianandrea Noseda, Roman Simovic & David Cohen
programme.desc: Italian conductor Gianandrea Noseda leads the combined forces of the Music Academy KestonMAX fellows and London Symphony Orchestra (LSO) in Ludwig van Beethoven's Triple Concerto in C major, Op. 56, and Hector Berlioz's Symphonie Fantastique, Op. 14. Beethoven's Concerto for Violin, Cello, and Piano in C major, commonly known as the Triple Concerto, was composed from 1803 to 1804. The choice of the three solo instruments effectively makes this a concerto for piano trio, and it is the only concerto Beethoven ever completed for more than one solo instrument. Berlioz's Symphonie Fantastique (‘Episode in the Life of an Artist … in Five Sections') is a programmatic symphony written in 1830, telling the story of a gifted artist who, in the depths of hopelessness and despair because of his unrequited love for a woman, has poisoned himself with opium. This concert was recorded at LSO St. Luke's in London, UK, on November 20, 2022.

programme.title: Myung-whun Chung, Orchestra del Maggio Musicale Fiorentino, Coro del Maggio Musicale Fiorentino & Christiane Karg
programme.desc: Maestro Myung-Whun Chung leads the Orchestra and Chorus of the Maggio Musicale Fiorentino, along with two vocal soloists, in this impressive performance of Gustav Mahler's Symphony No. 2 in C minor, ‘Resurrection'. Soprano Christiane Karg and alto Claudia Huckle are the soloists. Written between 1888 and 1894, this five-movement symphony is scored for a large orchestra, chorus, and soprano and alto soloists. Mahler explores themes of life, death, and resurrection throughout the symphony. Its first movement resembles a funeral march, while the second, a graceful Ländler, creates a stark contrast with the somber character of the first. The third movement is a Scherzo based on Mahler's musical setting of ‘Des Antonius von Padua Fischpredigt' from the collection Des Knaben Wunderhorn. The composer also used another Wunderhorn poem, ‘Urlicht' (Primal Light), for the fourth movement, incorporating both text and music, sung by the alto. The chorus makes its entrance in the second part of the fifth and final movement with the ‘Resurrection' chorale. This performance was recorded at Teatro del Maggio Musicale Fiorentino in Florence, Italy, in 2021.

programme.title: Les Dissonances & David Grimal
programme.desc: Les Dissonances is a collective of artists founded by violinist David Grimal in 2004. The conductorless ensemble consists of musicians from the most prestigious European orchestras, international soloists, and young talents. In this performance, Les Dissonances play Wolfgang Amadeus Mozart's Serenade No. 10 for winds in B-flat major, K. 361, also known as the ‘Gran Partita'. The composition is written for twelve winds and double bass and consists of seven parts. The piece's duration is longer than the composer's other chamber music works. Remarkably, Mozart chose to use a larger instrumentation. To the eight instruments of the common wind octet, Mozart added two basset horns, two additional horns, and a double bass. This performance was recorded at Philharmonie de Paris, France, in 2015.

programme.title: Vasilii Andreev
programme.desc: The award-winning Imperial Ice Stars are 26 Olympic, World, European and National Championship skaters, who jointly hold over 250 competition medals, performing theatrical ice dancing on a frozen theatre stage. The Imperial Ice Stars have received five-star praise from critics and standing ovations from audiences at some of the world's most prestigious venues - London's Royal Albert Hall and Sadler's Wells, Singapore's Esplanade Theatre, Montreal's Place des Arts and Cape Town's Artscape. This stunning performance, filmed at the Melbourne Theatre Company in Australia, offers a new look on the secular tale of Sleeping beauty, with the original music of Pyotr Ilyich Tchaikovsky (1840-1893).

programme.title: Ludwig Wicki, Danish National Symphony Orchestra, DR Junior Choir & Andrea Lykke Oehlenschlæger
programme.desc: Swiss conductor Ludwig Wicki leads the Danish National Symphony Orchestra and the DR Junior Choir in a sparkling concert program of film music. The program features a selection of music from great and Oscar-winning Hollywood films, including Titanic, The Bodyguard, Pirates of the Caribbean, Braveheart, and Moulin Rouge, written by major composers such as John Williams, Hans Zimmer, James Horner, and Klaus Badelt. Highlights include ‘He's a pirate/Davy Jones' from Pirates of the Caribbean: Dead Man's Chest ‘Now We Are Free' from Gladiator ‘My Heart Will Go On' from Titanic and ‘Shallow' from A Star Is Born. Featured soloists in this concert are the young talented singers Andrea Lykke Oehlenschlæger and Diluckshan Jeyaratnam. This performance was recorded at the DR Koncerthuset in Copenhagen, Denmark, in May 2022.

programme.title: Peter Gabriel
programme.desc: PETER GABRIEL n English singer-songwriter, musician and humanitarian activist who rose to fame as the original lead singer and flautist of the progressive rock band Genesis. After leaving Genesis in 1975, Gabriel went on to a successful solo career, with “Solsbury Hill” his first single. His 1986 album, So, is his most commercially successful, and is certified triple platinum in the UK and five times platinum in the U.S. The album's biggest hit, "Sledgehammer", won a record nine MTV Awards at the 1987 MTV Video Music Awards, and it remains the most played music video in the history of MTV. Using interviews, news archive and performance this episode reveals the unique aspect of this iconic artist.

programme.title: Ocean Vibes

programme.title: Ocean Vibes

programme.title: Flying Over Iceland 2
programme.desc: "Flying Over Iceland II: Summer in the Golden Circle" presents a serene aerial view of Iceland's south coast, starting at Skogafoss and moving to Vik, with black sand beaches, cliffs, and Vatnajökull glacier. Filmed in 2020, this 1-hour film features local wildlife and immersive music.

programme.title: Flying Over Kauai
programme.desc: "Flying Over Kauai" provides a soothing aerial journey over Hawaii's Garden Island, featuring the lush NaPali Coast, waterfalls, canyons, and beaches. Paired with new music by The Cynic Project and nature sounds, this 4K film offers a visually and sonically immersive experience.

programme.title: Flying Over Oahu
programme.desc: "Flying Over Oahu" offers a 97-minute aerial journey over Oahu's iconic coastlines with nature sounds and music by Alex Smith (The Cynic Project). From Waikiki Beach to Turtle Bay, enjoy breathtaking 4K landscapes, ending with a stunning Waikiki sunset.

programme.title: Flying Over Spanish Islands
programme.desc: In their first collaboration, cinematographer Stefan Zimmerman and Nature Relaxation™ producer David Huting present "Above the Spanish Islands." This 50-minute 4K aerial film captures Mallorca and the Canary Islands, set to ambient music by Diamans, Electus, and Michael St. Laurent.

programme.title: Flying Over Iceland
programme.desc: "Flying Over Iceland: Part I" provides a soothing aerial journey through eastern Iceland's fjords and valleys, starting at sunrise in Brei_dalsvik. Enjoy views of mists, a waterfall, and the Brei_dals' river, set to nature sounds and music by The Cynic Project, showcasing East Iceland's beauty.

programme.title: Kapas Biru Waterfall (Music)
programme.desc: Escape to Indonesia with tropical vegetation and a spectacular rushing waterfall.

programme.title: Nacpan Beach
programme.desc: Travel to a beautiful stretch of cream-colored sand with swaying coconut palms and clear waters.

programme.title: Ninety Mile Beach: New Zealand
programme.desc: Explore the almost never-ending paradise of one of New Zealand's most breathtaking beaches.

programme.title: Fall in the Forest 2
programme.desc: Fall in the Forest is an ultra-relaxing two-hour long dynamically changing film which sweeps viewers from one golden yellow autumn mountain scene to the next. Containing nature sounds only, it's a perfect video to turn on and add the beauty and warmth of Autumn to any environment. Filmed in the mountains of Alberta: Banff & Jasper National Parks, as well as in King's Canyon National Park, in California

programme.title: Gentle Creek: British-Columbia
programme.desc: Admire the twin summits, lush valley, and sparkling creek of Golden Ears Provincial Park in British-Columbia.

programme.title: Golden Hour in Paradise w Music
programme.desc: This 1-hour long epic 4K UHD video collage with soft instrumental music sweeps viewers from one golden moment in paradise to the next, with ambient nature sounds and soft music for deep relaxation. Viewers will travel to the corners of the earth on a Nature Relaxation journey par excellence. From mystic mountains to soothing seas, this film has a little piece of paradise for everyone.

programme.title: Nusa Penida: Indonesia (Music)
programme.desc: Travel to Bali and enjoy a breathtaking bird's eye view of the lush coastline.

programme.title: Featured Artists—Scheff + Moy
programme.desc: A fusion of the diverse artistic visions of our featured artists. Marc Scheff captures emotions through expressive portraits that speak to the soul while Mischelle Moy embraces the beauty of nature through vibrant colors and mesmerizing landscapes.

programme.title: Featured Artists—Winsy + Rise
programme.desc: A fusion of the diverse artistic visions of our featured artists. Cole Rise mesmerizes with otherworldly landscapes bathed in soft hues while Kate Winsy invites viewers into a serene visual journey where nature's beauty takes center stage.

programme.title: Happy Hour—Oktoberfest
programme.desc: Immerse yourself in the lively spirit of Oktoberfest and the camaraderie of happy hour. With a blend of vivid colors and intricate details, this art collection transports you to bustling beer gardens, traditional attire, and clinking glasses.

programme.title: Happy Hour—Part 4
programme.desc: This art collection masterfully captures life's vibrancy, alive with the exuberant energy of happy hour. Each piece dances with color and movement complementing the refreshing and illuminative mid-tempo tunes of strings, keys, synth leads, and bass.

programme.title: Latin Lens—Part 2
programme.desc: From bold murals to evocative photographs, this collection illuminates the rich heritage and unique perspectives of Latin American artists. The melodies are bright, and the instruments feature guitars, keys, horns, synths, bongos, and shakers.

programme.title: Autumn Hues—Part 1
programme.desc: Immerse yourself in a kaleidoscope of fall foliage, where trees dance in radiant shades and leaves gently cascade in the crisp breeze. This assortment of artworks celebrates the rich palette of warm hues, including burnt oranges, deep reds, and earthy browns.

programme.title: Moon Festival—Part 1
programme.desc: Indulge in a rich tapestry of art, a celebration of the ethereal beauty of the full moon. This vibrant collection weaves together hues that dance with lunar radiance, invoking a profound appreciation for abundance and gratitude

programme.title: Moon Festival—In Motion
programme.desc: A mesmerizing motion art collection capturing the magical beauty of the full moon, evoking feelings of abundance and gratitude. These luminous works celebrate the moon's serene glow, inspiring reflection on nature's cycles and the richness of life's moments.

programme.title: Halloween Party—Part 1
programme.desc: Bold colors and haunting melodies bring out the magic of Halloween, inviting viewers to revel in the merriment of the holiday. From grinning skulls to moonlit scenes, the collection enchants with its lighthearted take on spooky.

programme.title: Tranquil Impressions—Part 2
programme.desc: Experience a serene and evocative art collection designed to provide a peaceful retreat from the chaos of daily life. This collection inspires calm, wonder, and awe, offering a soothing escape that captivates the soul and rejuvenates the spirit.
